text: Before starting a project, setting clear goals is essential. A common and effective method is using SMART goals. This means your goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. Once your overall goal is defined, break it down into smaller milestones. These are key checkpoints that help you track progress along the way. You should also list your deliverables—the tangible results or products you will complete at each milestone. Finally, make sure you have stakeholder alignment. Everyone involved, from team members to clients, should agree on the goals, milestones, and final deliverables. This prevents confusion and keeps the project moving forward smoothly.
